column94: Study-type gate: primary study with new sequencing and IVCT phenotyping on 235 individuals (case series) [Results/Individuals sections]. PS4 mode: proband_count per ACMG/AMP generic guidance (Table 3, PS4 Note 2), which allows counting multiple unrelated affected probands for rare variants when case-control statistics are not available. Variant identification: The CACNA1S variant of interest (p.Arg174Trp; c.520C>T) is explicitly reported in the document. Exact strings observed include “c.520C>T” and “R174W” with genomic coordinate “chr1:201091993” and rsID “rs772226819” (variant tables and Table 2B). The INPUTS also list 1:201061121G>A; both genomic coordinates are recorded as aliases due to potential build/reference differences. Phenotype mapping: Allowed phenotype is Malignant hyperthermia (Autosomal dominant). The cohort comprises individuals referred for MH evaluation; IVCT was performed on all, yielding classifications of malignant hyperthermia susceptible (MHS) or non-susceptible (MHN). The paper reports that variants of unknown significance (VUS) were found in 21 individuals and were subdivided into 19 MHS and 2 MHN; however, it does not attribute specific MHS/MHN status to each listed VUS per-variant in the provided text. Counting: Because the document does not explicitly link the CACNA1S c.520C>T (R174W) carrier(s) to MHS status, we cannot confirm affected status for any independent proband with this variant. Therefore, under proband_count, a=0 (no confirmed affected+variant-positive), and b=c=d are not applicable and set to 0. Estimation policy: Although the variant appears in the VUS list, the affected-only minimum rule (a≥1) requires that the variant be listed among affected cases. Here, per-variant phenotype is not specified; thus we cannot assert affected status and do not apply the minimum count. Independence: Family structure and independence for any CACNA1S c.520C>T carrier(s) are not described; default assumption of relatedness would apply if multiple carriers were present, but no counting was possible due to absent per-variant phenotype mapping. Controls: No unrelated healthy control arm specific to this variant is provided; no b/c/d entries derived.

column120: A point system is used to tally probands (Antithrombin deficiency PP4/PS4 Rule Specification Guidance, PS4 section): 1 point is awarded for each proband with an antithrombin activity level of < 0.8 IU/mL (or below the lab's reference range) confirmed on repeated independent samples. 1 point is awarded for each proband with an antithrombin activity level of < 0.8 IU/mL without repeat confirmation but with a family history of antithrombin activity levels < 0.8 IU/mL. 1 point is awarded for each proband with an abnormal crossed immunoelectrophoresis assay demonstrating decreased antithrombin function. 0.5 points are awarded for each proband with an antithrombin activity level of < 0.8 IU/mL without confirmation of repeated independent samples tested. Strength levels are assigned based on total proband points: PS4_Supporting: 1 point; PS4_Moderate: 2-3 points; PS4 (Strong): 4-7 points; PS4_VeryStrong: 8 or more points.

column127: Study-type gate: This is a primary cohort/genotyping study of SERPINC1 with individual- and genotype-level aggregation (case series with internal subgrouping) (Methods; Results; multiple tables). Therefore counting proceeds. PS4 mode: The SERPINC1 VCEP requires proband-based counting for PS4 (ClinGen Thrombosis Expert Panel Specifications; Antithrombin deficiency PP4/PS4 Rule Specification Guidance), so we tally unrelated probands only. Variant identification and alias reconciliation: The variant of interest SERPINC1 NM_000488.4:c.235C>T (p.Arg79Cys) appears multiple times using standard HGVS and legacy names. Table 1 lists '235C>T' at amino-acid position 79, mutation 'Arg79Cys', deficiency type IIHBS, with counts; the paper and Table 3 also refer to the heparin-binding-site variant group 'HBS Padua', a historical name corresponding to p.Arg79Cys at codon 79 ('HBS mutations at position 79' text; Table 3 HBS Padua row). We therefore treat 'AT Padua'/'HBS Padua' as equivalent to p.Arg79Cys and include these in variant_forms. Phenotype mapping: Allowed phenotype per GUIDE is 'antithrombin III deficiency' (autosomal dominant). The study ascertained subjects with suspected inherited AT deficiency (AT heparin cofactor activity ≤80%) and then confirmed SERPINC1 mutations (Methods). Thus carriers included in the genotype subgroups (e.g., HBS Padua) have a laboratory phenotype consistent with antithrombin deficiency; mean AT heparin cofactor activity for HBS Padua was 53.2% (range 40–65), i.e., <0.8 IU/mL (Table 3). Testing was performed at distance from anticoagulant and hormonal treatments (Methods), addressing key PS4 inclusion criteria. Counting and independence: For Arg79Cys (AT Padua), Table 3 explicitly reports 'Subjects=50' and 'Probands=32; Family members=18' (row 'HBS Padua'). Per the SERPINC1 PS4 proband-count framework, we count unrelated probands only. The 'Probands=32' field provides the exact number of independent probands with the variant and laboratory phenotype, so a=32. We did not use the larger 'Number of families=45' from Table 1 because Table 3 provides the more specific proband count for this genotype subgroup; using probands is conservative and aligns with the VCEP definition of independence. Controls/unaffected are not required in proband_count mode and none are tallied here (b=c=d=0). Exclusions and notes: The cohort contains both probands and relatives; we avoided double counting by using the explicit proband number. Inclusion criteria on anticoagulant status and pregnancy/oral contraceptive exposure were handled by study design (testing at distance), and all included subjects in this subgroup had AT activity below reference, satisfying the laboratory phenotype. We cannot ascertain repeat sampling; under the VCEP rubric, such cases would still accrue 0.5 points each if repeats were not documented, but we do not assign points in this counting-only step. The paper excluded other biological VTE risk factors and cataloged cardiovascular risk factors; there is no explicit statement about excluding acquired AT deficiency (e.g., liver disease), but the genetic ascertainment and consistent HBS phenotype support constitutional deficiency; this is noted for reviewer awareness.

column131: Workflow summary: 1) Determined PS4 mode as proband_count per SERPINC1 VCEP guidance. 2) Identified the variant in the document using HGVS and legacy names; 'AT/HBS Padua' corresponds to p.Arg79Cys (c.235C>T). 3) Mapped phenotype to allowed 'antithrombin III deficiency' using the study’s ascertainment (AT cofactor ≤80%) and genotype-based grouping. 4) Counted unrelated probands directly from Table 3 (HBS Padua: Probands=32), ensuring independence and avoiding family-member double counting; set a=32; b=c=d=0. 5) Noted study handling of anticoagulant/hormonal exposure; repeated sampling not specified (points not assigned in this step). Self-critique adjustment: Initially considered using 'Number of families=45' from Table 1 as an independence proxy; upon review, Table 3 provides explicit proband counts for HBS Padua, so we adopted the conservative and guideline-concordant value of 32 probands. No overlapping cohorts within the paper required further deduplication. The final tally is total_ps4_case_counts=32.
